review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they address the need to store audit logs on separate systems, which aligns with the control's requirement. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern, but minor adjustments were made for clarity and consistency.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'stored_separately' instead of 'storage_separation' for clearer intent
title: Protection of Audit Information | Store on Separate Physical Systems or Components
step1_reasoning: The control AU-9 (2) requires audit logs to be stored on separate physical systems or components to ensure their protection. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that audit logs are stored externally from the cluster, such as in a dedicated logging service or external storage solution. This separation helps protect the integrity and availability of audit logs.
